steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The first sentence states that more diverse behaviors correlate with a larger, more energy-demanding brain. The second sentence claims that animals performing only basic actions should allocate fewer resources to brain tissue. The blank must signal that the second claim follows as a logical consequence of the first — not a time sequence, an addition, or a contrast.

steps.title: Eliminate each choice and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• "Subsequently,": signals that the second claim occurs later in time than the first — but this is a consequence, not a chronological follow-up. ✗
  • "Besides,": signals that the second claim is a separate, additional point alongside the first — but it is a direct consequence, not a parallel addition. ✗
  • "Nevertheless,": signals contrast, implying the second claim is true despite the first — but the two claims align, not oppose each other. ✗
  • "Thus,": signals “therefore,” marking the second sentence as a logical consequence of the correlation stated in the first. ✓
That matches the correct answer — D is correct.
